title: Tech Debt Cleanup Initiative: Aether Package
description:
## Summary
This is a meta-task to track the tech debt cleanup effort for the aether package. These tasks address code quality, error handling, security, and maintainability issues identified through code review.

## Tasks

### Critical/High Priority
- **at-cac42c83**: Fix excessive unwrap() usage (114 instances) - @engineer-1
- **at-ca98c68a**: Fix CSRF token handling in OAuth integration - @engineer-1
- **at-1dd02e54**: Properly handle errors in main.rs - @engineer-1

### Medium Priority
- **at-1de786e5**: Consolidate error types across crates - @engineer-3
- **at-4a5f6530**: Consolidate duplicate mappers logic across LLM providers - @engineer-2
- **at-56b4eb3b**: Fix unwrap() in agent spawning with graceful error handling - @engineer-2

### Low Priority
- **at-1df52efe**: DRY up duplicate shutdown logic in MCP manager - @engineer-2
- **at-4a54ca57**: Streamline stream import patterns across modules - @engineer-3
- **at-b5533cf1**: Organize module structure - @engineer-1
- **at-56c0939c**: Add tracing/instrumentation for observability - @engineer-3

### Dependencies
```
at-1dd02e54 ┐
            ├─> at-56b4eb3b   (agent error handling depends on main.rs error handling) 
at-cac42c83 ┘

at-1de786e5 ──> at-ca98c68a     (OAuth security work needs consistent error types)
```

## Overall Impact

### Code Quality
- **~114 unwrap() calls** removed → better error handling
- **Error types standardized** → easier debugging
- **~40-60 lines of duplicated code** removed → better maintainability
- **Module organization** improved → better code navigation
- **Stream imports** consolidated → developer experience

### Security
- **CSRF protection** improved → OAuth security
- **Token handling** hardened → credential protection

### Observability
- **Tracing/structured logging** added → debugging capability
- **Better error messages** → user experience

### Maintainability
- **Common patterns extracted** → easier to add new LLM providers
- **Consistent code style** → easier onboarding

## Estimated Effort
- **Total lines of code affected**: ~500-1000 lines
- **Estimated engineering time**: 2-3 weeks total across team
- **Testing effort**: Medium (ensure no regressions)
- **Risk**: Low (mostly refactoring with good test coverage)

## Recommended Approach
1. Start with high-priority security and unwrap() fixes
2. Do error type consolidation as a foundation
3. Parallelize medium and low priority tasks
4. Run full test suite after each task
5. Consider code review guidelines for tech debt cleanup

## Success Metrics
- [ ] Zero unwrap() calls remaining (verified via grep)
- [ ] All TODO comments addressed
- [ ] Consistent error types across modules
- [ ] Tracing implemented in core modules
- [ ] No new clippy warnings
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Code coverage maintained or improved

## Notes
- These are non-functional changes that improve code quality and maintainability
- No breaking API changes intended (except where noted in specific tasks)
- Consider bundleing related changes in single PRs to minimize disruption
